sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Newcastle United have struggled recently, with no wins in their last 9 matches and a tendency for low-scoring games (less than 2.5 goals in 4 of 5). Liverpool, on the other hand, have shown strong attacking form, scoring consistently with over 2.5 goals in their last 7 matches, though they've failed to keep a clean sheet in 12 consecutive games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, results are split with Newcastle winning 2-1 at Liverpool and Liverpool securing a 2-0 home victory. Both teams often score in these encounters (4 of 5 recent games), with matches tending to have over 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Newcastle's defensive stats are solid, ranking 1st for least goals conceded, but their attack struggles, with no goals scored yet this season. Liverpool, positioned 5th in the league, have a potent attack (4 goals in recent games) but defensive vulnerabilities, conceding in every match.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Trends
sections.text: Liverpool's Mohamed Salah has been exceptional against Newcastle, averaging a 7.92 Sofascore rating with 4 goals and 4 assists in the last 5 matchups. Newcastle's aerial strength (66.7% duels won, league rank 1) could pose a challenge to Liverpool's defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Liverpool lead Newcastle United 1-0 at St James' Park. The solitary goal came from Ryan Gravenberch in the 35th minute, giving the away side the edge. Newcastle have shown promise in attack but failed to convert their chances, while Liverpool capitalized on a key opportunity. The home side's task is made tougher with a red card to Anthony Gordon, leaving them with 10 players for the second half. Discipline has been an issue for both teams, with Newcastle picking up a yellow card and Liverpool receiving two.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Newcastle United's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Newcastle United have shown attacking intent throughout the first half, creating a couple of big chances but failing to find the back of the net. Their expected goals reflect a decent level of threat, yet poor finishing has let them down. Harvey Barnes has been a creative force in midfield, while players like Sandro Tonali have shown glimpses of danger. However, the red card to a key player is a massive blow, reducing their numbers and likely impacting their defensive structure and attacking output. Defensively, they’ve held firm for most of the half but were undone by a well-taken goal.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Liverpool's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Liverpool have been clinical when it mattered, taking the lead through Gravenberch's strike. Despite creating fewer big chances than their opponents, they made the most of their opportunity. Their expected goals are lower, suggesting they’ve been less threatening overall, but their efficiency in front of goal has been the difference. Defensively, they’ve been relatively solid, with players like Virgil van Dijk and Alisson stepping up when needed. Possession has been slightly in their favor, allowing them to control portions of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Newcastle started brightly with consistent attacking spells in the opening minutes, pushing forward with intent. Around the midway point, Liverpool began to assert themselves with a notable shift in momentum, building pressure that eventually led to their goal. Towards the end of the half, there were moments where Newcastle regained some attacking rhythm, but Liverpool also had significant spells of dominance. Overall, the momentum was fairly balanced, with both teams having periods of control.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key challenges for second half
halftimeSections.text: Newcastle face a significant challenge in the second half, playing with a man down after the red card. Their ability to reorganize defensively while maintaining an attacking threat will be crucial. Converting their chances is a must if they are to get back into the game. For Liverpool, the focus will be on exploiting the numerical advantage and managing the game to protect their lead. They’ll likely look to slow the tempo at times while seeking to capitalize on counter-attacks or set pieces. Discipline will also be key, as further cards could shift the dynamics.
